fans.description: Ceiling fans evaluated on airflow efficiency and long-term motor durability.

motor_type.note: DC motors use up to 70% less energy than AC motors and run quieter.

cfm_rating.note: CFM (Cubic Feet per Minute) measures airflow. For rooms over 200 sq ft, target ≥5000 CFM.

blade_pitch_degrees.note: Blade pitch affects how much air is moved per revolution. 12–15° is the sweet spot.

noise_level_db.note: Anything above 50 dB will be audible in a quiet room.

warranty_years.note: Motor warranty is the most important — look for a separate motor warranty.

mounting_type.note: Ceilings below 8 ft require a flush (hugger) mount for safety.

lighting.description: Light fixtures evaluated on color quality, efficiency, and longevity.

cri.note: CRI (Color Rendering Index) measures how accurately a light renders colors. Below 80 makes food and skin look unnatural. Kitchens and bathrooms should be ≥90.

lumens_per_watt.note: Higher lumens-per-watt = more efficient. LED fixtures should exceed 80 lm/W.

dimming_compatible.note: Verify compatibility with your existing dimmer switch brand — many LEDs are not universally dimmable.

hardware.description: Kitchen and bathroom hardware evaluated on finish durability and dimensional accuracy.

material.note: Solid brass and 304 stainless steel are the most durable for wet environments.

finish.note: PVD finishes carry a lifetime warranty from most major manufacturers.

center_to_center_mm.note: Always measure center-to-center of existing hole spacing before ordering. Metric sizing is increasingly common on imported cabinetry.

warranty.note: Any hardware marketed for kitchen/bath use should carry a lifetime finish warranty.

flooring.description: Flooring evaluated on wear resistance, moisture tolerance, and installation complexity.

wear_layer_mil.note: For LVP/LVT: wear layer determines scratch and dent resistance. Under 6 mil is unsuitable for high-traffic areas.

ac_rating.note: For laminate flooring. AC3 handles moderate residential traffic; AC4+ for heavy use or light commercial.

moisture_resistance.note: For bathrooms and kitchens, ensure the CORE is waterproof — not just the surface layer.

installation_type.note: Floating installations allow for easier future replacement and are DIY-accessible.

cabinet_storage.description: Cabinet storage solutions evaluated on weight capacity, spatial efficiency, and hardware quality.

pullout_weight_capacity_lbs.note: Under-sink and pantry pull-outs holding pots and canned goods need ≥100 lbs capacity.

drawer_slide_type.note: Full-extension slides give 100% access to the drawer. Undermount slides are invisible and easier to level.

soft_close.note: Soft-close hinges and slides dramatically extend cabinet box life by preventing slamming.

box_material.note: Plywood boxes resist moisture and hold screws better than particleboard. Critical in kitchen and bath.

usable_volume_ratio.note: Measure interior usable depth vs. total cabinet depth. Factory cabinets often waste 20–30% of depth behind the face frame.
